title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 9: Number Theory and Logic
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 125 + 237 - 89?
Which of the following numbers is a prime number?
questions.solution: 21 = 3 x 7, 27 = 3^3, 33 = 3 x 11, 39 = 3 x 13. Only 29 has no divisors other than 1 and itself.

questions.question_text: A square has a side length of 7 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='140' height='140' viewBox='0 0 140 140'><rect x='25' y='25' width='90' height='90' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='60' y='18' font-size='12'>7 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with side length 7 cm.
questions.solution: Perimeter of a square = 4 x side = 4 x 7 = 28 cm.

questions.question_text: What is the next number in the sequence 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ...?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='260' height='60' viewBox='0 0 260 60'><text x='10' y='35' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The sequence 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ? displayed in a row.
questions.solution: Differences are 3, 5, 7, 9, so the next difference is 11. 26 + 11 = 37.

questions.question_text: Nina has 48 stickers. She gives one third to her brother. How many stickers does she have left?
questions.solution: One third of 48 is 16. She has 48 - 16 = 32 stickers left.

questions.question_text: A pitcher holds 2 liters of juice. How many milliliters is that?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='120' height='140' viewBox='0 0 120 140'><path d='M35 20 L85 20 L90 120 L30 120 Z' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='35' y1='50' x2='85' y2='50' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/></svg>
questions.solution: 1 liter = 1000 mL, so 2 L = 2000 mL.

questions.question_text: Tom is older than Lily. Lily is older than Max. Max is older than Eve. Who is the youngest?
questions.solution: The order from oldest to youngest is Tom > Lily > Max > Eve, so Eve is the youngest.

questions.question_text: How many vertices does a pentagon have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='140' height='130' viewBox='0 0 140 130'><polygon points='70,15 120,45 120,95 70,120 20,95 20,45' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.solution: A pentagon has 5 vertices.

questions.question_text: If 6 apples cost 18 dollars, how much do 10 apples cost at the same rate?
questions.solution: One apple costs 18 / 6 = 3 dollars. Ten apples cost 10 x 3 = 30 dollars.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of the digits of the number 4837?
If 5x - 3 = 22, what is the value of x?
questions.solution: Add 3 to both sides: 5x = 25. Divide by 5: x = 5.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has an area of 48 square cm and a width of 6 cm. What is its length?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='200' height='100' viewBox='0 0 200 100'><rect x='20' y='25' width='160' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='185' y='55' font-size='12'>6 cm</text><text x='90' y='55' font-size='12'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with width 6 cm and unknown length.
questions.solution: Length = Area / width = 48 / 6 = 8 cm.

questions.question_text: Liam has 3 different shirts and 4 different pairs of pants. How many outfits can he make?
questions.solution: For each shirt there are 4 choices of pants: 3 x 4 = 12 outfits.

questions.question_text: In a class, the ratio of boys to girls is 3:5. If there are 15 boys, how many girls are there?
questions.solution: 3 boys correspond to 5 girls. 15 boys = 5 groups of 3, so 5 x 5 = 25 girls.

questions.question_text: What is the greatest common divisor of 36 and 60?
A triangle has angles measuring 50 degrees and 70 degrees. What is the measure of its third angle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='180' height='130' viewBox='0 0 180 130'><polygon points='30,110 150,110 90,20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='35' y='105' font-size='11'>50</text><text x='135' y='105' font-size='11'>70</text><text x='85' y='35' font-size='11'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle with two angles of 50 and 70 degrees.
questions.solution: Angles in a triangle sum to 180 degrees. 180 - 50 - 70 = 60 degrees.

questions.question_text: On a table there are red, blue, and green cards. The red cards and blue cards together total 14. The blue and green together total 11. The red and green together total 13. How many blue cards are there?
questions.solution: Add all three: 2(R+B+G) = 38, so R+B+G = 19. Then B = 19 - (R+G) = 19 - 13 = 6.

questions.question_text: Triangular figures grow so that figure 1 has 1 dot, figure 2 has 3 dots, figure 3 has 6 dots, and each new figure adds a row with one more dot than the previous row. How many dots are in figure 6?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='220' height='120' viewBox='0 0 220 120'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='70' cy='20' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='60' cy='40' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='80' cy='40' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='15' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='130' cy='35' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='150' cy='35' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='120' cy='55' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='55' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='160' cy='55' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><text x='15' y='80' font-size='11'>1</text><text x='65' y='80' font-size='11'>2</text><text x='135' y='80' font-size='11'>3</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Figure 1 has 1 dot, figure 2 has 3 dots arranged as a triangle, figure 3 has 6 dots arranged as a larger triangle.
questions.solution: Triangular numbers: 1, 3, 6, 10, 15, 21. Figure 6 has 21 dots.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 2:45 PM and lasts 1 hour and 50 minutes. At what time does it end?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='120' height='120' viewBox='0 0 120 120'><circle cx='60' cy='60' r='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='60' y2='20' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='88' y2='74' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='55' y='15' font-size='10'>12</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A clock face showing an analog time.

questions.question_text: A swimming pool is being filled at a rate of 15 liters per minute. How many liters are added in 1 hour and 20 minutes?
questions.solution: 1 h 20 min = 80 min. 80 x 15 = 1200 liters.

questions.question_text: How many three-digit numbers have all their digits odd?
questions.solution: Each of the three digit positions can be 1, 3, 5, 7, or 9. Total = 5 x 5 x 5 = 125.

questions.question_text: A large square is divided into 4 equal smaller squares. The perimeter of each small square is 20 cm. What is the area of the large square?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='160' height='160' viewBox='0 0 160 160'><rect x='20' y='20' width='120' height='120' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='140' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='140' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large square divided into four smaller equal squares.
questions.solution: Small square side = 20/4 = 5 cm. Large side = 2 x 5 = 10 cm. Area = 10^2 = 100 square cm.

questions.question_text: Four friends each either always tell the truth or always lie. Anna says 'Beth lies.' Beth says 'Cara lies.' Cara says 'Dan lies.' Dan says 'Anna and Beth are both liars.' How many of them are liars?
questions.solution: Suppose Anna tells the truth. Then Beth lies, so Cara's claim 'Cara lies' is reversed: Cara tells the truth, so Dan lies. Dan's statement that Anna and Beth both lie is indeed false since Anna tells the truth, consistent. Liars: Beth and Dan, so 2 liars.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive integers is 81. What is the largest of these integers?
questions.solution: If the middle number is n, sum = 3n = 81, so n = 27. The largest is 27 + 1 = 28.

questions.question_text: In how many different ways can the letters of the word KANGA be arranged in a row?
questions.solution: KANGA has 5 letters with A repeated twice. Arrangements = 5! / 2! = 120 / 2 = 60.

questions.question_text: A shape is made of a square with side 6 cm with a right triangle attached to one side. The triangle has legs 6 cm and 4 cm. What is the total area of the shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='200' height='140' viewBox='0 0 200 140'><rect x='20' y='30' width='80' height='80' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='100,30 100,110 140,110' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='50' y='75' font-size='11'>6 cm</text><text x='110' y='125' font-size='11'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A 6 cm square with a right triangle of legs 6 and 4 cm attached to its right side.
questions.solution: Square area = 36. Triangle area = (6 x 4) / 2 = 12. Total = 36 + 12 = 48 square cm.

questions.question_text: The product of two positive integers is 96 and their sum is 20. What is the larger of the two numbers?
questions.solution: Find two numbers with sum 20 and product 96: 8 and 12 since 8 + 12 = 20 and 8 x 12 = 96. The larger is 12.

questions.question_text: A sequence starts 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, 16, ..., where each term is obtained by adding one more than was added previously. What is the 12th term?

Five houses stand in a row, numbered 1 to 5 from left to right. The yellow house is in position 1. The white house is in position 3. The green house is immediately to the left of the blue house. Which color is the fifth house?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='260' height='90' viewBox='0 0 260 90'><rect x='10' y='25' width='40' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='60' y='25' width='40' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='110' y='25' width='40' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='160' y='25' width='40' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='210' y='25' width='40' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='20' y='85' font-size='11'>1</text><text x='70' y='85' font-size='11'>2</text><text x='120' y='85' font-size='11'>3</text><text x='170' y='85' font-size='11'>4</text><text x='220' y='85' font-size='11'>5</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of five houses numbered 1 through 5 from left to right.
questions.solution: Positions 1 = yellow and 3 = white. The remaining colors (red, green, blue) fill positions 2, 4, 5. Green must be immediately left of blue, so green and blue occupy adjacent positions. The only adjacent pair among {2, 4, 5} is (4, 5), giving green = 4 and blue = 5. The leftover color red goes in position 2. Therefore the fifth house is blue.

questions.question_text: A mixture contains water and syrup in the ratio 7:3. If 20 liters of the mixture are taken, how many liters of syrup does it contain?
questions.solution: Total parts = 7 + 3 = 10. Syrup fraction = 3/10. Syrup in 20 L = 20 x 3/10 = 6 liters.
